ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Continued To Nov-16-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adam Lazar for (petitioner) Herb Thomas.

Mr. Lazar reported that they did not submit an amended petition as in the petitioner's view there was nothing to amend, that the accounting is full and complete, that they did a forensic accounting regarding the missing assets, that one payment was to close out the account, that another had been sold and put into the account prior to the appointment and that for the 3rd they are able to determine that it was not in the account at the time and that the previous trustee submitted an accounting ending in 2012. The Court stated that it wants the accounting amended with proper dates and carry values and that they are being given one last chance to get it right. Mr. Lazar stated that Mr. Thomas is doing his best to comply with the court, that they were able to track down prior statements and that the asset was not there. The Court stated that in terms of form and dates there is a date discrepancy between the prior ending date and this starting date and that we are at an impasse because of the lack of clarification of the next steps. Mr. Lazar stated that Mr. Thomas was appointed in February of 2013. The Court stated that it needs an amended accounting that starts with 11/1/12. Mr. Lazar stated that Mr. Thomas is only able to identify assets as he received them in 2013 but that they would comply. The accounting petition was continued to November 16, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

The court asked about the missing bonds and the amended accounting. Mr. Lazar reported that they are carrying the value of the bonds, that the previous trustee died prior to being able to determine when the bonds were purchased and that the transaction identified where the funds were withdrawn from the closed account and put into the trust. The court stated that there is an issue with the time period. Mr. Lazar stated that this is due to the transition, that the period cannot be accounted for, that they have subpoenaed the bank records and that beyond that determining fair market value is not available. The court stated that it has serious concerns regarding the discrepancies, that it will have a discussion with the examiner and that this matter will be continued for Mr. Thomas to file an amended petition. Off Calendar Oct-21-2008 Continued To Jan-28-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Raymond Levy in Propria Persona. Bruce Feder for Samuel Bell (Beneficiary). Dennis Vann for Linda Harrington (Conservator).

Mr. Levy stated that he was not kept informed with the issues in regard to the Beneficiary and that the Trust was in his discretion. Mr. Vann stated that Mr. Levy had been the Trustee for 8 years and that Mr. Bell was homeless during that time. In addition, Mr. Vann reported that with the help of Ms. Harrington, Mr. Bell was no longer homeless, that he was attending City College, that he had medical and that he had an SSI appointment in November. Furthermore, Mr. Vann questioned why Mr. Levy felt the need to deny Mr. Bell of his rights. Mr. Feder stated that Mr. Bell was safe and content at the Granada, that he was spending alot of time defending Mr. Bell's rights and stated that the trust could make payments without affecting SSI. In addition, Mr. Feder stated that Mr. Levy needed to understand his obligation to Mr. Bell or let someone else be appointed as Trustee. The matter was continued to January 28, 2009 at 10:30 a.m. for status. The Court ordered Mr. Levy to continue paying the bills. A report is due by January 20, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing`
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Continued To Aug-05-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann with for and with Linda Harrington. Raymond Levy in Pro Per (Trustee). Bruce Feder for Sam Bell (Beneficiary of the trust).

Ms. Harrington reported that the medical application was still pending, that Mr. Bell was registered at City College and that the GGRC hearing was continued to July 23, 2008. In addition, she stated that Mr. Bell continued to thrive and that she was prepared to provide the receipts to Mr. Levy. Mr. Levy stated that he received a dental estimate not a receipt, that SSI should have been applied for first and that he did not pay the assessment fees as the trust was not Mr. Bell's asset. He also stated that his client was not obligated to Mr. Bell, that the trust was for education only and that it should not be tapped into until all other benefits were exhausted. Mr. Feder stated that it was not a special needs trust, that it was a support trust, that it would not intefere with Mr. Bell's benefits and that Mr. Levy needed to provide for Mr. Bell's support. The Court will allow time for Mr. Vann to respond to the documents filed by Mr. Levy. Ms. Harrington will present the receipts to Mr. Levy. The Court ordered that Mr. Levy has an obligation to provide for Mr. Bell. The matter was continued to August 5, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR#4609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Sam Bell (Beneficiary). Raymond Levy in Pro Per. Dennis Vann for and with Linda Harrington (Conservator).

Ms. Harrington reported that Mr. Bell had been assessed, that Mr. Bell was not in the category to obtain services from the Golden Gate Regional Center (GGRC) and that she had contacted Counsel for further assistance in this matter. In addition, she stated that Mr. Bell was in secured housing, that she was looking into Mr. Bell attending City College, that Mr. Levy had been kept informed and that Mr. Bell needed to be approved for Medical first before he could apply/qualify for SSI. Mr Levy stated that he had not received the budget he requested, had not obtained the psychological report and that Mr. Bell could be placed in a non-luxury type housing. Ms. Harrington will provide the dental bill to Mr. Levy. Mr. Vann will file the budget. The Court ordered that Mr. Bell is to remain in the current housing (The Granada). Mr. Levy will prepare the order and present it to all counsel. The matter was continued to June 10, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: B. Mann.
